desc: Gothenburg is Sweden's softer, saltier second city: a tram-and-canal west-coast hub where seafood, design, brewery bars, Haga cafés and easy archipelago ferries give it a more relaxed rhythm than Stockholm. It suits travellers who want a city break with water, food and neighbourhood character rather than monument-chasing intensity.

todo.name: Southern archipelago ferries
todo.desc: Ride public ferries to car-free islands for granite coast, swimming spots and a slower west-coast rhythm.

todo.name: Feskekorka and seafood
todo.desc: Use the fish-market area and harbour restaurants to make seafood a centrepiece of the trip.

todo.name: Liseberg
todo.desc: Plan around seasonal openings, from summer rides to the winter lights and Christmas market.

todo.name: Haga district
todo.desc: Walk cobbled streets, browse small shops and split an oversized cinnamon bun.

todo.name: Stenpiren or Saltholmen archipelago jump-off
todo.desc: Treat the ferry system as part of the city experience, not a side detail, because Gothenburg's island access is one of its biggest advantages.

todo.name: Lindholmen or city-sauna evening
todo.desc: Balance the postcard districts with one more contemporary waterside area if you want the full range of Gothenburg's personality.

wifi.note: Excellent across hotels, cafés, trams and much of the city centre, so Gothenburg is easy to navigate and re-plan from on the fly; ferry days are the only times it feels less frictionless.

prac.fltNote: Landvetter makes Gothenburg easy to use, and Swedavia's airport information confirms London is one of its nonstop markets even though the destination-level LGW and LCY flags remain false here. In practice the city is simple once you land; the bigger price swings come from summer event traffic, Liseberg peaks and long-light weekend demand rather than from difficult transfers.
prac.visa: Schengen rules apply; UK/Irish travellers should verify 90-in-180-day limits and passport validity before travel.
alerts: Archipelago ferry frequency is better in the warmer season.
Rain jackets matter; west-coast showers can arrive quickly.
Major events can make central hotels expensive for a city of this size.

sweet: May to August is usually Gothenburg's best stretch for archipelago ferries, outdoor seafood terraces and those very long Scandinavian evenings, with June and early September especially good if you want light without absolute peak pressure.
